office_does: A state representative writes and votes on state laws and budgets, conducts oversight, and represents their legislative district.
race_blurb: Eight-year incumbent Debra Lekanoff faces first-time legislative candidate Cindy Carter, with differences on taxes, fiscal policy, environment, and reproductive rights.

candidates.summary: Carter is a first-time candidate focused on repealing the state income tax, fiscal restraint, transportation, and resident control.
highlights: Supports repealing the statewide income tax [S1][S2]
Calls for fiscal responsibility and returning power to residents [S1]

taxes.basis: She explicitly supports repealing the statewide income tax.

spending.basis: She explicitly calls for fiscal responsibility.

experience.basis: She has no documented elected record.

local-control.basis: She explicitly calls for returning government power to residents.

candidates.summary: Lekanoff is an eight-year representative emphasizing public investment, environmental protection, reproductive freedom, and a broad public-service record.
highlights: Supports housing, health care, schools, unions, and jobs [S1]
Supports reproductive freedom and environmental protection [S1]

spending.basis: Her platform emphasizes public investment in housing, health care, schools, and transportation.

climate.basis: She explicitly supports environmental protection and has a documented climate and salmon-recovery record.

experience.basis: She is an eight-year incumbent with more than 30 years of public-service work.
